topic: SAA Exponential Cell Separation — c3 patch verification
change_summary: Decider direct patch addressed c3 holes 1+2+3: (1) finding field rewritten — removed unverified "Author concedes method invalid" framing, replaced with mechanism-vs-rate honest text; (2) code_analysis.monitoring changed live_fetch → hardcoded, eliminating WIN-004/WIN-005 cross-tag inconsistency; (3) finding no longer overstates Terra-Nova et al. as first-principles deriving the rate. detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/detail_claim/TLDRs unchanged from c3.
our_argument_summary: Std Model Explains. MHD core dynamics (Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PEPI) provide the spatial mechanism for SAA bifurcation via reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary; westward drift ~0.2–0.3°/year is flow-driven advection in a rotating spherical shell; asymmetric African-vs-American decay is attributed to the African LLSVP altering local core flow. Dome has no MHD equations and no mechanism for azimuthal asymmetry. The 30.8→50.6° bifurcation rate is acknowledged as observational on both sides — Terra-Nova et al. do not specifically derive it, and the dome publishes no tau for its 'exponential' label. Verdict backed by post-hoc trap (CHAOS-7 publicly available, no independent timestamp pre-registration).

relevant_files: raw-text/06-evolution.txt lines 16, 20-21 — earlier-version concessions about "failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting" (NOT a current-method concession)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 138, 148, 162, 228-229 — 20-domain list mentions "SAA decay" rate (77 nT/yr), not angular separation
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Confirmed: dome's automation live-fetches a DECAY RATE (≥28 nT/year threshold at SAA-region INTERMAGNET stations, mapping to WIN-037/042). The angular separation value (50.57°) is hardcoded in 05-model.txt as an architectural constant. PRED-013's 120-180° by 2055 target is not auto-monitored — 30-year-horizon manual claim. No live-fetch pipeline for angular separation exists in the dome's published code references.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 50.57° is hardcoded. Two endpoints (30.8° in 2000, 50.6° in 2025) define the dome's claimed curve; the 'exponential' label has no published tau and is not distinguishable from linear/quadratic/log fits with only two points. The dome has staked no falsifiable functional form.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020) — official coverage to ~2020
INTERMAGNET station data — for live decay-rate monitoring (different WIN family: 037/042)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: If 2055 separation falls below dome's 120° lower bound: dome can invoke 'aetheric damping' (no public tau commits the dome to a specific shape). If above 180°: claim 'overshoot.' Between 120-180°: PRED-013 confirmed. The unpublished tau is the central escape mechanism.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: RESOLVED in c4. The c3 review flagged that monitoring='live_fetch' was unsupported by raw-text evidence and inconsistent with WIN-005 (same data bundle, hardcoded). Decider direct patch applied the c3 recommendation: WIN-004 monitoring is now 'hardcoded'. WIN-004/WIN-005 now share monitoring='hardcoded', relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false — fully consistent. Aggregate counts in Part 2b use computed placeholders ({{CA_HARDCODED}}/{{CA_LIVE}}) so the prose auto-updates; no stale numerator survives. Independently confirmed: only 2 wins (006, 007) remain tagged 'live_fetch' across all 71 — that aggregate now has narrative weight and WIN-006/007 deserve scrutiny in a future cycle, but that is out of scope for WIN-004.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: 05-model.txt line 43 lists "SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg" as a static architectural parameter alongside other model constants (sun altitude, moon altitude, firmament height). The 20-domain live-fetch list (08-live-power.txt) tracks "SAA decay" — a rate metric (77 nT/yr) that maps to WIN-037/042. No live pipeline exists for angular separation. WIN-005 (same Terra-Nova citation, same CHAOS-7 dataset, tightly coupled bifurcation/asymmetry phenomenon) is also hardcoded — full cross-WIN consistency.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: SAA bifurcation is explained by standard geomagnetism as the surface expression of reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PEPI). The dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric vortex decay' without changing any predicted value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020) documented SAA bifurcation in public literature before the dome model incorporated it. PROS-001 claims 'prospective' status but the 30.8°/50.6° bracket was readable from CHAOS-7 snapshots at the time of the dome commit. No timestamped pre-registration in a venue independent of the author was located.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The dome's PROS-001 and WIN-004 text uses the label 'exponential separation' / 'decay curve of the aetheric vortex' but publishes no tau, no functional form, and no derivation path from dome geometric parameters (disc radius 20,015 km, firmament height 9,086 km, aether coupling κ). 'Exponential' here is a label without a derivation.

holes_found.description: Honesty creates a defender pivot point that has not been preempted in the summary row. The new finding states "bifurcation rate in detail evidence is observational, not first-principles-derived." This is true and the right move scientifically — but a capable dome defender will pivot exactly on it: "Both sides retrofit the rate; the dome at least named 'exponential' before 2025; therefore the post-hoc critique cuts both ways." The c3 advocate_mode flagged this defense as defense_survives=4 (high resilience) and recommended a preemptive sentence in detail_verdict_text closing the symmetry door (LLSVP-asymmetry argument, mechanism-existence-vs-rate distinction). That preemption was NOT integrated when the finding was patched. The c3 critique was now MORE prominently located (summary row, visible without expanding) but the c3 defense WAS NOT moved with it. A reader who never expands the panel sees the symmetric concession but not the asymmetric kill-shot.
holes_found.recommendation: Add ONE sentence to detail_verdict_text (immediately after the first paragraph, before the post-hoc trap block): "The mechanism gap is asymmetric: globe-model MHD provides reversed-flux patches and LLSVP-modulated core flow that were published before CHAOS-7 data arrived, while the dome publishes a label without a tau — and the African-vs-American asymmetry is fatal to a radially symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of prediction timing." This recovers the rhetorical weight that the "concedes method invalid" sentence used to carry.

holes_found.description: Carryover from c3 hole #4: detail_claim still reads "Globe models have no mechanism for SAA splitting or asymmetric decay" with no attribution prefix. This is the dome's assertion, not ours, but reads as our own concession. Decider c3 patch did not address this field.
holes_found.recommendation: Prefix with "The dome model asserts that" or wrap the claim in quotes: "The dome model asserts: 'Globe models have no mechanism for SAA splitting or asymmetric decay.'" Trivial fix; preempts a rhetorical gotcha.

holes_found.description: Unsourced supporting claim in detail_evidence. The text says the bifurcation rate "is consistent with westward-drift advection in broader MHD models" but cites no specific paper for this consistency. Terra-Nova et al. is cited only for the spatial mechanism. A defender could ask "name the MHD paper that publishes the 0.8°/year separation rate prediction" and our text has no answer — not because the claim is wrong, but because we did not source it.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a citation for an MHD paper that publishes a comparable separation rate (Aubert 2015 "Geomagnetic forecasts driven by thermal wind dynamics" or Wardinski et al. 2008 SAA westward-drift work are candidates worth checking), or (b) soften to "is consistent with the westward-drift advection regime modeled by MHD" — drops the implicit promise that a specific paper exists.

holes_found.description: TLDR_verdict's flat assertion "The data was publicly available before the prediction" lacks an in-page anchor for the timing chain. Detail_verdict_text contains the chain (CHAOS-7 publicly available, monitor commit on same line as data fetch, no independent timestamp), but the TLDR makes the flat claim without pointing readers to where they can verify it. Not a factual error — a documentation gap that a TLDR-only reader cannot resolve.
holes_found.recommendation: Either add 'see post-hoc analysis below' to the TLDR or accept the gap (TLDRs are simplifications by design — see CLAUDE.md TLDR review guidance). Marking minor; do not block on this.

stronger_arguments.description: PROMOTE LLSVP AZIMUTHAL ASYMMETRY INTO SUMMARY. The c3 stronger_arguments flagged this and the c3 patch did not pick it up. The radially-symmetric aetheric vortex vs. observed African-vs-American asymmetry is the cleanest falsification available and — critically — is timing-independent (immune to the post-hoc symmetry pivot identified in hole #1). Adding it to the summary-row finding converts "globe has more mechanism" into a structural geometric falsification: a radially symmetric mechanism cannot produce azimuthal structure. Proposed expansion to finding: "... dome has no fluid mechanism; bifurcation rate in detail evidence is observational, not first-principles-derived; African-vs-American decay asymmetry falsifies a radially symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of prediction timing."

PREDICTION-CONTENT TEST FOR 'EXPONENTIAL'. With only two endpoints (2000, 2025), exponential / linear / quadratic / logarithmic fits are mathematically indistinguishable; the dome publishes no tau. Adding this single sentence to detail_extra or detail_verdict_text converts the dome's bold-sounding 'exponential' label into a demand the dome cannot meet without abandoning rhetorical flexibility: "The dome's 'exponential' label specifies no tau and no functional form; with only two endpoints (2000, 2025) it is not distinguishable from linear, quadratic, or logarithmic curves until ~2035." This was c3's #1 stronger argument and remains uintegrated.

PRED-013 FEASIBILITY MATH (carry-over from c3). 50.6° in 2025 → 120-180° by 2055 requires 2.31-4.31°/year vs. observed ~0.79°/year — i.e. 2.9-5.5× the current rate. Add the arithmetic explicitly in detail_extra: shows the dome IS willing to stake a long-horizon bet (credit) but the current rate has not started accelerating toward the band (debit). Use 30 years (2055-2025) and rate = (50.6-30.8)/25 = 0.792°/year — the c3 review caught and corrected the c2 off-by-one, do not propagate the c2 number.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's c3 patch is a substantive improvement. Removing the unsourced "Author concedes method invalid" line eliminates a citation failure that a defender could have used to discredit the entire WIN entry. Changing the monitoring tag to 'hardcoded' fixes a real cross-WIN inconsistency that violated our own CLAUDE.md guidance ("Never assume the previous reviewer got it right"). The new finding's admission that the bifurcation rate is observational on both sides demonstrates intellectual honesty — exactly the credibility move that distinguishes our review from the dome's own writing. The dome model genuinely identifies SAA bifurcation as a real, scientifically interesting phenomenon, and the patched finding now treats this fact fairly.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real phenomenon does not validate a proposed mechanism. MHD provides a causal mechanism (reversed-flux patches at CMB, LLSVP-modulated flow) where the dome provides only a label without a tau and a radially symmetric geometry incompatible with observed azimuthal asymmetry. PRED-013's feasibility math (2.9-5.5× rate acceleration required by 2055) means the dome's most testable long-horizon commitment is currently consistent with, but not predicting, the observed trajectory. The verdict 'Std Model Explains' remains warranted: the standard model has the mechanism; honest acknowledgment that neither side has first-principles-derived the SPECIFIC RATE does not change which side has a mechanism.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender, after the c3 patch, would now pivot exactly on the new honest framing: "Your own finding now admits the bifurcation rate is observational on both sides — Terra-Nova et al. don't derive it, and we don't either. So both models are post-hoc on the rate. The post-hoc trap therefore cuts both ways. We at least published 'exponential' as a qualitative claim before 2025; you have no MHD paper that staked a quantitative rate prediction in advance. And you are equivocating between EXISTENCE of a mechanism (which MHD has — flux patches) and PREDICTIVE POWER of that mechanism for the specific 30.8→50.6° trajectory (which neither model has). Until a globe-MHD paper publishes a pre-registered separation rate, the post-hoc critique cannot single us out, and we are the only side that has staked a long-horizon prospective claim (PRED-013)." This is the same defense the c3 review flagged as defense_survives=4, with the additional sting that the new finding makes it more textually grounded.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Move c3's recommended preemptive sentence into detail_verdict_text — see hole #1 above. The single most effective addition is the LLSVP-asymmetry argument because it is timing-independent (closes the symmetry door entirely): a radially symmetric aetheric vortex cannot produce azimuthal structure regardless of when the prediction was made. Folding LLSVP into the summary-row finding (per stronger_argument #1) is the highest-leverage edit available.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: RESOLVED in c4. WIN-004 and WIN-005 now both have monitoring='hardcoded', relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false. WIN-023 (SAA formation ~950 AD) and WIN-024 (Roaring 40s) and WIN-035 (African cell intensity threshold) are also tagged consistently with our 'no MHD mechanism' framing across the SAA cluster. No remaining inter-WIN tag inconsistency in the SAA bundle. Note for future cycles: only WIN-006 and WIN-007 remain tagged 'live_fetch' across all 71 wins — that aggregate is small enough that any future reclassification will materially shift Part 2b's narrative weight; both deserve scrutiny in a future spot-check.

claims_checked: aggregate live_fetch count after WIN-004 reclassification
quantitative_verification.computations: (50.6-30.8)/25 = 0.792°/year, rounds to 0.8°/year — matches our text. Linear extrapolation to 2055 (30 years): 50.6 + 0.792*30 = 74.4°. Required rate for PRED-013 lower bound (120° by 2055): (120-50.6)/30 = 2.31°/year = 2.92× current rate. Required rate for upper bound (180°): 4.31°/year = 5.45× current rate. Dome's hardcoded 50.57° matches observed 50.6° to 0.03° — essentially identical, consistent with a value read off CHAOS-7 rather than derived. Live-fetch aggregate after c3 patch: 2/71 wins (WIN-006 and WIN-007 only); 28/71 hardcoded; 41/71 none — totals to 71, internally consistent. Finding character count = 195, matches text_fingerprint.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None on this run. c3 stronger-arguments arithmetic correction (74.4° not 70.6°) was already documented in c3 holes_found and was not propagated into the published text — clean.

deeper_analysis: This run is a verification pass, not a fresh excavation. The decider's direct patch did the right things: removed an unsourced claim, fixed a real cross-WIN tag inconsistency, and softened an overclaimed citation. All three c3 major/moderate holes are demonstrably addressed. The remaining issues are second-order: c3 hole #4 (detail_claim attribution) was a minor and survives; a new moderate emerges from the patch itself, in that the rhetorically-loaded 'concedes method invalid' anchor has been replaced with a balanced concession ('rate is observational on both sides'), which is more honest but creates a defender pivot the c3 advocate_mode already mapped — and the c3-recommended preemption (LLSVP asymmetry, mechanism-vs-prediction distinction) was not integrated alongside the patch. The single highest-leverage edit available now is to fold the LLSVP-asymmetry argument into either the finding or detail_verdict_text, because it is timing-independent and immune to the symmetry pivot. Recommended action 'minor_edit' rather than 'major_rewrite': the WIN's substantive position is correct, the detail evidence is bulletproof, and the gaps are textual rather than analytical.

citations_verified: DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 — Terra-Nova et al. (2017) 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches,' PEPI. (DOI was wrong in c2, fixed in c3, still correct in c4.)
raw-text/05-model.txt line 43 — confirms 'SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg' is hardcoded as an architectural constant (validates new monitoring='hardcoded' tag).

raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 16 — confirms only an EARLIER-VERSION concession exists ('failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting' at v0.4); NO concession that the current method is invalid. Validates removal of the 'Author concedes method invalid' claim from c3-prior text.
raw-text/02-wins.txt PROS-001 — confirms dome publishes no tau; only the qualitative label 'exponential separation curve.'
raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 138/148/162/228 — confirms the 20-domain list tracks SAA DECAY rate (77 nT/yr), not angular separation; validates absence of live-fetch pipeline for separation.
citations_unchecked: https://john09289.github.io/predictions live-site link in detail_verdict_text — not checked against archive (not blocking).
Implicit appeal to 'broader MHD models' for westward-drift advection in detail_evidence — no specific paper cited; flagged as minor hole #3.
